language.title: Language
language.description: Choose the language used across the dashboard.

page.title: Settings
page.subtitle: Manage your preferences, team, and instance configuration

github.title: GitHub
github.requiresCloud: Requires Openship Cloud — the App is owned by openship.io
github.connectPrompt: Connect your GitHub account to deploy repositories
github.disconnectAppBody: This removes the Openship OAuth account row. The GitHub App installation stays until you uninstall it on GitHub.
github.cloudExplainer: The Openship GitHub App is owned by openship.io. Connect your instance to Openship Cloud to use scoped install tokens for cloning private repos (works for local AND remote deploys).
github.linkExplainer: Link your GitHub account to import repositories, enable auto-deploy on push, and manage branches directly from the dashboard.

ghCli.title: gh CLI
ghCli.fallbackDesc: Optional local-build fallback for repos outside your App installations
ghCli.localOnlyTitle: Refused at deploy time for remote-server builds. The App or a per-project clone token is required for remote deploys.
ghCli.usedForDeploysTitle: No cloud connection — gh CLI is the primary source for everything right now
ghCli.activeWarnStrong: gh CLI is the active source.
ghCli.activeWarnRest: Deploys to remote servers will be refused — connect the Openship App or set a per-project clone token to deploy to remote targets.
ghCli.primaryNotePrefix: Openship App is the primary source. gh CLI fills in for
ghCli.primaryNoteSuffix: against repos outside your App installations.
ghCli.optionalSuffix: on this machine to enable local-build clones of repos outside your App installations. Remote deploys always route through the App.

deployDefaults.title: Deploy Defaults
deployDefaults.description: Where new deployments should land by default
deployDefaults.intro: Picked target is preselected on the deploy picker. You can still override it per deployment, or clear it to be asked every time.
deployDefaults.noServers: No servers configured yet - add one from the deploy picker.

buildPreferences.title: Build Preferences
buildPreferences.description: Control how your projects are built and deployed
buildPreferences.intro: Choose the default build strategy for new deployments. Per-project overrides are available in project settings.

cloneCredentials.title: GitHub clone credentials
cloneCredentials.description: Personal token used to clone private repos when the GitHub App isn't installed
cloneCredentials.intro: Paste a GitHub personal access token (classic or fine-grained). The clone module uses it as the second-priority credential - after any per-project override, before the GitHub App installation token.
cloneCredentials.scopeHintPrefix: For private repos, give the token the
cloneCredentials.scopeHintSuffix: scope (classic) or fine-grained read access to the repos you want to deploy.
cloneCredentials.useAsDefaultDesc: When on, the clone module uses this token as the second tier in the chain (after per-project overrides). When off, the token is stored but not used - handy if you only want it active for specific deploys.

toast.notLikeToken: That doesn't look like a GitHub token. Save anyway if you're sure.

tokens.title: Personal Access Tokens
tokens.description: Bearer tokens for the API, CLI, and MCP clients. Each token acts as you.
tokens.explainer4: scopes a token to exactly what you choose — even below your own role. Ideal for a narrow MCP token.
tokens.onlyTime: This is the only time it's shown. Store it somewhere safe.

scopePicker.title: Token scope
scopePicker.subtitle: The token is limited to exactly these resources. You can only grant access you hold yourself.

toast.pickResource: Pick at least one resource, or turn off scoping

mcp.title: MCP
mcp.description: Connect AI agents to your Openship API over the Model Context Protocol.
mcp.bannerStrong: Paste the endpoint below into your MCP client
mcp.bannerRest: (Claude, Cursor, …) — it opens a browser window where you approve access and choose what the client can reach. No token to copy. Openship is a standards-compliant OAuth 2.1 MCP server.
mcp.endpointNote: Streamable-HTTP JSON-RPC. OAuth-capable clients authorize in the browser; on approval you pick read-only + which resources the client may access.
mcp.oauthNote: The client opens a browser to authorize — you approve access and pick what it can reach. Nothing to copy.
mcp.tokenNote: For clients without OAuth, or when you want a fixed, scoped token in the config.
mcp.createTokenSuffix: . A read-only token limits the agent to reads.

team.heading: Team
team.descPersonal: People with access to your personal workspace. Invite collaborators directly, or create a separate team organization.
team.descTeam: People with access to this organization's projects, deployments, and servers.

createTeamCard.title: Create a team organization
createTeamCard.body: You can invite people here directly — or spin up a separate shared space that stays isolated from your personal projects.

createTeamModal.title: Create team organization
createTeamModal.body: Your personal workspace stays untouched. The team org has its own projects, servers, and invited members.

memberPanel.subtitle: Grants apply to restricted members. Owner/admin/member have org-wide access by default.

team.confirmRemove: Remove this member from the organization?

workspace.deleteBody: Permanently remove this workspace and everything in it.
workspace.leaveBody: Leave this workspace — you'll need a new invite to return.
workspace.deleteConfirm: Delete this workspace? Its projects, members, and settings are removed for everyone. This can't be undone.
workspace.leaveConfirm: Leave this workspace? You'll lose access until you're invited again.

manage.title: Manage workspace
manage.sectionBody: Rename this workspace, pause its projects, or delete it.
manage.pauseBody: Stop every project but keep the workspace — resume anytime.
manage.deleteBody: Tears down all {count} projects (containers and routes) and removes the workspace. Volume data is left recoverable. Can't be undone.

toast.deleteBillingActive: Cancel this workspace's active subscription before deleting it.

inviteMember.headerDescRestricted: Set their role on the left, then pick exactly what they can reach on the right.
inviteMember.headerDescDefault: We'll email them a link to join this organization.
inviteMember.roleMemberDesc: Read + write to all resources in this organization.
inviteMember.roleAdminDesc: Everything Member can do, plus manage members + billing.
inviteMember.roleRestrictedDesc: No default access — pick exactly which resources to unlock.
inviteMember.pickerDesc: Restricted members start with no access. Pick what this invite unlocks once accepted — you can grant more later from the member's row.

toast.personalOrgBlocked: Personal workspaces can't invite members. Create a team organization first, then send invitations.

teamWorkspace.title: Invite your team
teamWorkspace.description: Move this workspace somewhere your teammates can reach.
teamWorkspace.body: This workspace lives only on this computer, so teammates can't reach it and invites are off. Move it to a shared location — your own server, Openship Cloud, or a tunnel — to start inviting people.
teamWorkspace.ownerOnlyTitle: Only the workspace owner can migrate
teamWorkspace.comingSoon: Linking directly to a remote instance is coming soon.

cloud.featuresActive: Cloud deployments, managed SSL, and global CDN are active for your instance.
cloud.unlockDesktop: Unlock cloud deployments, managed SSL, and global CDN.
cloud.deployLocal: Deploy to cloud directly from your local instance.
cloud.confirmDisconnect: Disconnect from Openship Cloud? Cloud deployments will stop working until you reconnect.

upgradeAuth.title: Add a password
upgradeAuth.description: Promote this zero-auth instance to a real email + password login. Your projects and history stay intact.
upgradeAuth.name: Name
upgradeAuth.useMailServerDesc: Send password resets, verifications, and team invites from your provisioned mail server instead of the env-based SMTP fallback.

toast.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
toast.accountCreated: Account created — you are now signed in

channels.title: Delivery channels
channels.description: Where your notifications go. Verified channels are reachable; unverified ones are skipped.
channels.empty: No channels yet. Add one to start receiving notifications.
channels.confirmDelete: Delete this channel? Subscriptions using it will stop.

subscriptions.title: What to be notified about
subscriptions.description: Pick which events go to which channel. Each cell is one subscription.
subscriptions.empty: Add a channel first to subscribe to events.

orgDefaults.title: Organization defaults
orgDefaults.description: Defaults applied when a new member joins this organization. Members can override per-channel.

audit.heading: Audit log
audit.subtitle: Every change in this organization, who made it, and when.
audit.name: Name

updates.title: Updates
updates.description: How Openship checks for and applies new releases
updates.autoUpdateDesc: Download and install new releases automatically. Off by default — you stay in control of when your app changes.
updates.notificationsDesc: Show a notice when a new release or a security advisory affects your version. Critical security advisories are always shown once, even when this is off.
updates.securityDescription: Full transparency over what talks to your install
updates.security1: Openship checks for updates and security advisories
updates.securityOnlyGithub: only from the public GitHub releases
updates.security4: this data — no third-party service and no Openship-operated server can push updates or signals to your install. Advisories are pinned to each release, so unreleased changes never reach you. Critical security advisories are always shown once; everything else respects the toggle above.

export.title: Export data
export.description: Download the entire instance database as a file.
export.intro: Creates a portable snapshot of everything — projects, servers, deployments, settings. Set a passphrase to include your secrets (env vars, tokens, SSH credentials); they are re-encrypted under it, so the file is safe to move. Leave it blank to export without secrets.
export.toastWithSecrets: Export downloaded. Keep the passphrase safe — you'll need it to import.
export.toastNoSecrets: Export downloaded. Secrets were NOT included (no passphrase set).

import.title: Import data
import.description: Restore an exported file onto this instance.
import.intro: Load a file exported from another Openship install. Enter the passphrase used when it was exported to restore secrets too.
import.modeReplaceDesc: Wipe this instance and restore the snapshot exactly. Recommended for migrating to a new machine.
import.modeMergeDesc: Keep current data and add the imported data. Your own account and settings are preserved.
import.wipeWarn: This erases all current data on this instance and signs you out.
import.confirmWipe: Replace ALL data on this instance with the imported snapshot? This cannot be undone, and you'll be signed out.
import.notExport: That doesn't look like an Openship export file.
import.cantRead: Could not read that file — is it a valid export?
import.secretsNotRestored: secrets NOT restored (no/incorrect passphrase) — re-enter credentials

migrate.chooseIntro: Right now this instance is single-user. Pick how you want your teammates to reach it.

cards.serverBody: SSH-deploy openship on a VPS you own. Data moves there. Best for teams that already have infrastructure.
cards.cloudBody: Push your data to api.openship.io. We host everything. Best for teams that want zero infrastructure.
cards.tunnelBody: Your data stays on this machine. We provision an Oblien edge tunnel so teammates can reach you over the public internet — your machine has to be online.
cards.tunnelWarn: Tunnels are provisioned through your cloud account.

server.noServersPrefix: No servers configured. Add one in
server.successDetail: openship is now running on your server. Project id {projectId}.

cloud.connectFirstBody: Open Settings → Cloud and sign in to api.openship.io. Then come back here to migrate.
cloud.whatHappens: What happens when you click migrate
cloud.bullet1: Your local DB is dumped (encrypted fields stripped — credentials don't leave this machine).
cloud.bullet2: The dump is uploaded to api.openship.io under your active org.
cloud.bullet3: Local instance becomes a launcher pointing at app.openship.io.
cloud.bullet4: You can switch back any time — your local data stays on disk.
cloud.proceedNonEmpty: Proceed even if my cloud org already has projects (I'll handle conflicts)
cloud.toastConflict: Your cloud org already has {count} projects. Tick the box to proceed and include this instance's data alongside what's already there.

tunnel.connectFirstBody: Tunnels are provisioned through Openship Cloud. Open Settings → Cloud and sign in, then come back here.
tunnel.bullet1: Your data stays on this machine. Nothing moves.
tunnel.bullet2: Teammates can reach you only when this machine is online.
tunnel.bullet3: The tunnel agent runs inside the API process — no extra service to babysit.
tunnel.slugHint: 3-32 chars, lowercase letters, digits, dashes. Must start and end with a letter or digit.
tunnel.successDetail: Tunnel "{slug}" is publishing your local dashboard.
tunnel.toastNeedsCloud: Tunnels need your cloud account connected. Open Settings → Cloud first.

switchBack.nothingBody: This instance is already in single-user mode.
switchBack.resultLocalTunnel: Local mode restored. Tunnel torn down — your data was never moved.
switchBack.resultLocalGrace: Local mode restored. Remote data left intact for the 30-day grace period.
switchBack.relinkBody: Encrypted columns are stripped on cross-host moves. Re-link these before they're needed again:
switchBack.heading: Switch back to single-user
switchBack.intro: Pull this instance out of {mode} and put it back in solo mode on this machine.
switchBack.warnTunnel: The tunnel will be torn down. Anyone using the public URL will get connection refused.
switchBack.warnRemote: The remote keeps your data for 30 days as a grace period in case you change your mind.
switchBack.dontPull: Don't pull remote data — keep whatever is on this machine right now.
switchBack.dontPullDesc: Use this when the remote is unreachable or you intentionally want to discard remote changes.
switchBack.toastRemoteUnreachable: Remote unreachable. Tick "don't pull remote data" to switch back anyway.
